frameworks.name: Bayesian observer model
frameworks.one_line: Ideal observer infers latent hierarchical motion structure during scene perception.

frameworks.name: hierarchical motion structure
frameworks.one_line: Formal framework characterizing nested form-motion relations among stimulus features.

frameworks.name: multiple object tracking
frameworks.one_line: Established attention paradigm used as testbed for structured motion.

core_question: Do humans actively employ knowledge of hierarchical motion structure when perceiving dynamic visual scenes?
key_assumptions: Real-world motion exhibits hierarchical form-motion relations
Observers can represent latent structure and combine it with sensory evidence
Bayesian inference is an adequate normative description of structured perception
manipulations: Structured vs independent object motion in tracking
Depth of motion hierarchy across conditions
Visibility: occluded vs visible object trajectories in prediction task
key_findings: Tracking performance was higher under structured than independent motion
Bayesian observer attributes gain to active structure use, not simpler stimuli
In occlusion-prediction task, humans inferred correct or near-correct structure
Structure inference held even for deep motion hierarchies
interpretations: Humans actively exploit hierarchical motion structure during perception
Structure knowledge is used inferentially under uncertainty, not just as stimulus regularity
